model_narrative: We schedule the jobs of a project over a horizon of discrete time periods. Each job takes a known number of periods to process and consumes given amounts of several renewable resources during every period it is active, with each resource limited to a fixed amount available per period. Some jobs cannot begin until other jobs have finished. For each job we decide the period in which it finishes, which fixes when it runs. The objective is to minimize the makespan, the total time needed to complete the entire project.

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the jobs of the project in topological order; the first job is a dummy super-source that starts the project and the last job is a dummy sink that ends it, both with zero duration, while the jobs in between are the real work activities

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the discrete time periods of the planning horizon in chronological order; the position of a period in this ordering is its clock time, so the first period is time 1, the second is time 2, and so on

sets.name: r
sets.doc: the renewable resources, each with a fixed amount available in every period

sets.name: pred
sets.doc: the precedence relations as ordered pairs of jobs; a pair means the first job must be completed before the second job may start

sets.name: x_index
sets.doc: the feasible (job, period) pairs; for each job this lists only the periods inside its finish-time window, the range of periods in which that job could possibly finish given precedence and the horizon length

params.name: durations
params.doc: the processing time of each job, in number of periods; once a job starts it occupies that many consecutive periods, and the two dummy jobs have duration zero

params.name: demands
params.doc: the amount of each renewable resource that a job consumes in every period during which it is active

params.name: capacities
params.doc: the amount of each renewable resource available in any single period

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the finish indicator, equal to 1 if the job finishes exactly in that period and 0 otherwise; defined only over the feasible job-period pairs

vars.name: makespan
vars.doc: the total project duration, equal to the time at which the whole project completes

description: Tie the total project duration to when the final dummy job completes. The makespan must equal the finishing time of the last job in the project, measured as the number of periods elapsed from the start of the horizon up to the period in which that job finishes.

description: Respect every precedence relation between jobs. For each ordered pair of jobs where the first must come before the second, the finishing time of the first job must leave enough room before the finishing time of the second, namely the second job cannot finish any earlier than the first job's finish plus the second job's own processing time.

Each job takes a known number of periods to process and consumes given amounts of several renewable resources during every period it is active, with each resource limited to a fixed amount available per period. Some jobs cannot begin until other jobs have finished. For each job we decide the period in which it finishes, which fixes when it runs. The objective is to minimize the makespan, the total time needed to complete the entire project.

params.name: durations

params.doc: the amount of each renewable resource available in any single period

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the finish indicator, equal to 1 if the job finishes exactly in that period and 0 otherwise; defined only over the feasible job-period pairs

vars.name: makespan
vars.doc: the total project duration, equal to the time at which the whole project completes

description: Keep resource use within capacity in every period. For each resource and each period, add up across all the real work jobs the amount of that resource consumed by jobs that are active in that period, where a job counts as active in a period if it finishes in that period or within its processing span afterward, and require this total to stay within the amount of the resource available per period.
expected_pyomo:
def resusage_rule(m, r, t):
    periods = list(m.t)
    ordt = {tt: ix + 1 for ix, tt in enumerate(periods)}
    from collections import defaultdict
    _tw = defaultdict(list)
    for (jj2, tt) in m.x_index:
        _tw[jj2].append(tt)
    jobs = list(m.j)
    actual = [jj for jj in jobs if jobs.index(jj) in range(1, len(jobs) - 1)]
    def fw(jj):
        dur = int(round(value(m.durations[jj])))
        lo, hi = ordt[t], ordt[t] + dur - 1
        return [tau for tau in _tw[jj] if ordt[tau] in range(lo, hi + 1)]
    return sum(m.demands[j, r] * sum(m.x[j, tau] for tau in fw(j)) for j in actual) <= m.capacities[r]
model.resusage = Constraint(model.r, model.t, rule=resusage_rule)

description: Schedule each job exactly once. For every job, exactly one of its candidate finishing periods must be chosen, so the job finishes in one and only one period.
expected_pyomo:
def once_rule(m, j):
    from collections import defaultdict
    _tw = defaultdict(list)
    for (jj, tt) in m.x_index:
        _tw[jj].append(tt)
    return sum(m.x[j, tt] for tt in _tw[j]) == 1
model.once = Constraint(model.j, rule=once_rule)

model_narrative: We schedule the jobs of a project over a horizon of discrete time periods. Each job takes a known number of periods to process and consumes given amounts of several renewable resources during every period it is active, with each resource limited to a fixed amount available per period. Some jobs cannot begin until other jobs have finished. For each job we decide the period in which it finishes, which fixes when it runs. The objective is to minimize the makespan, the total time needed to complete the entire project.

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the jobs of the project in topological order; the first job is a dummy super-source that starts the project and the last job is a dummy sink that ends it, both with zero duration, while the jobs in between are the real work activities

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the discrete time periods of the planning horizon in chronological order; the position of a period in this ordering is its clock time, so the first period is time 1, the second is time 2, and so on

sets.doc: the renewable resources, each with a fixed amount available in every period

sets.name: pred
sets.doc: the precedence relations as ordered pairs of jobs; a pair means the first job must be completed before the second job may start

sets.name: x_index

params.name: durations
params.doc: the processing time of each job, in number of periods; once a job starts it occupies that many consecutive periods, and the two dummy jobs have duration zero

params.name: demands

params.name: capacities
params.doc: the amount of each renewable resource available in any single period

vars.name: x

vars.name: makespan
vars.doc: the total project duration, equal to the time at which the whole project completes

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, tie the total project duration to the finishing time of the final dummy job, so the makespan equals how long the whole project takes to complete. Second, respect every precedence relation, so that whenever one job must come before another the later job cannot finish until enough time has passed for the earlier job to finish and the later job to run its full processing time. Third, keep resource use within capacity in every period, so that for each resource and each period the total consumed by all the real work jobs active in that period stays within the amount available. Finally, schedule each job exactly once, so every job finishes in one and only one of its candidate periods.
